perturbation.original_text: 21.1 Distributor shall obtain all licenses, permits and approvals required by any government, including any recycling or take-back programs applicable to packaging or Products, and shall comply with all applicable laws, rules, regulations, policies and procedures and any requirements applicable to the importation, exportation, use, sale, loan, purchase, and distribution of Products under telecommunications, consumer rights related, environmental, labor, tax, and any other laws and regulations, of any government or other competent authority where the Products are to be sold, used or deployed (collectively "Applicable Laws").
perturbation.changed_text: 21.1 Distributor shall endeavor to obtain licenses, permits and approvals required by any government, including recycling or take-back programs applicable to packaging or Products, and shall attempt to comply with all applicable laws, rules, regulations, policies and procedures and any requirements applicable to the importation, exportation, use, sale, loan, purchase, and distribution of Products under telecommunications, consumer rights related, environmental, labor, tax, and any other laws and regulations, of any government or other competent authority where the Products are to be sold, used or deployed (collectively "Applicable Laws").
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall obtain' to 'shall endeavor to obtain' and 'shall comply' to 'shall attempt to comply' introduces ambiguity and makes the obligation less strict. This creates uncertainty about the level of effort required for compliance.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Business and Professions Code § 17500 prohibits false or misleading advertising. By weakening the obligation to comply with 'all applicable laws', the distributor could potentially violate this section if their 'attempt' at compliance is insufficient, leading to misleading claims about their adherence to regulations in California, where ScanSource, Inc. does business.

GENERAL BUSINESS REGULATIONS [16000 - 18107] ( Division 7 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 61. ) PART 3. REPRESENTATIONS TO THE PUBLIC [17500 - 17943] ( Part 3 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 63. ) CHAPTER 1. Advertising [17500 - 17606] ( Chapter 1 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 63. ) ARTICLE 1. False Advertising in General [17500 - 17509] ( Article 1 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 63. ) 17500. It is unlawful for any person, firm, corporation or association, or any employee thereof with intent directly or indirectly to dispose of real or personal property or to perform services, professional or otherwise, or anything of any nature whatsoever or to induce the public to enter into any obligation relating thereto, to make or disseminate or cause to be made or disseminated before the public in this state, or to make or disseminate or cause to be made or disseminated from this state before the public in any state, in any newspaper or other publication, or any advertising device, or by public outcry or proclamation, or in any other manner or means whatever, including over the Internet, any statement, concerning that real or personal property or those services, professional or otherwise, or concerning any circumstance or matter of fact connected with the propo
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the distributor's obligation to comply with 'all applicable laws' by changing 'shall obtain' to 'shall endeavor to obtain' and 'shall comply' to 'shall attempt to comply'. This introduces ambiguity and makes the obligation less strict. The Law Explanation suggests this could potentially violate California Business and Professions Code § 17500 if the 'attempt' at compliance is insufficient, leading to misleading claims about adherence to regulations. Scraped Law Snippet 1 states, 'It is unlawful for any person, firm, corporation or association...to make or disseminate...any statement, concerning that real or personal property or those services...or concerning any circumstance or matter of fact connected with the propo'. By only attempting to comply, the distributor creates a higher risk of making statements that are false or misleading, leading to a legal contradiction as defined by the Ambiguity Legal Contradiction definition, because the change introduces an ambiguous obligation that makes it difficult to enforce compliance with state law.

perturbation.original_text: 17.1 With regard to both Distributor and Affiliates' activity, Distributor shall keep full, true, and accurate records and accounts, in accordance with generally-accepted accounting principles, of each Product or Service purchased and deployed, Resold, or distributed by serial number, including information regarding compliance with Cisco marketing and sales programs, Software usage, and export or transfer.
perturbation.changed_text: 17.1 With regard to both Distributor and Affiliates' activity, Distributor should keep reasonably complete records and accounts, in general alignment with accounting principles, of each Product or Service purchased and deployed, Resold, or distributed by serial number, including information regarding compliance with Cisco marketing and sales programs, Software usage, and export or transfer.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall keep full, true, and accurate records' to 'should keep reasonably complete records' and replacing 'generally-accepted accounting principles' with 'general alignment with accounting principles,' the obligation to maintain strict and reliable financial records becomes vague and subjective. The term 'general alignment' opens the door to discrepancies and incomplete data.
perturbation.law_explanation: 15 U.S. Code § 78m requires companies to maintain accurate books and records. The modified text, which only requires 'reasonably complete records' and 'general alignment' with accounting principles, is not in compliance with SOX, which emphasizes internal controls and transparent record-keeping. Since the contract involves financial transactions of a US-based company, this is a direct contradiction.

Periodical and other reports (a) Reports by issuer of security; contents Every issuer of a security registered pursuant to section 78l of this title shall file with the Commission, in accordance with such rules and regulations as the Commission may prescribe as necessary or appropriate for the proper protection of investors and to insure fair dealing in the security- (1) such information and documents (and suc
perturbation.justification: The modified contract introduces ambiguity regarding the accuracy of financial records, which contradicts the legal requirement for maintaining accurate books and records as described in the Law Explanation, referencing 15 U.S. Code § 78m. The original text mandated 'full, true, and accurate records,' while the changed text only requires 'reasonably complete records' and 'general alignment' with accounting principles. This weakening of the obligation undermines the legal expectation for precise and reliable financial documentation. Scraped Law Snippet 1, while primarily a list of historical amendments to 15 USC 78m, confirms the existence of this statute. The shift from mandatory ('shall') to advisory ('should') further diminishes the enforceability of the record-keeping obligation.

perturbation.original_text: 20.0 EXPORT RESTRICTIONS. Certain of the Products supplied by Cisco under the Agreement are subject to export controls under the laws and regulations of the United States (U.S.) and elsewhere. Distributor shall comply with such laws and regulations governing use, export, re-export, and transfer of Products and will obtain all required U.S. and local authorizations, permits, or licenses.
perturbation.changed_text: 20.0 EXPORT RESTRICTIONS. Certain of the Products supplied by Cisco under the Agreement might be subject to export controls under the laws and regulations of the United States (U.S.) and elsewhere. Distributor should consider complying with such laws and regulations governing use, export, re-export, and transfer of Products and may attempt to obtain required U.S. and local authorizations, permits, or licenses.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'are subject' to 'might be subject' introduces uncertainty regarding whether export controls apply. Changing 'shall comply' to 'should consider complying' and 'will obtain' to 'may attempt to obtain' weakens the mandatory nature of export compliance, making it optional.
perturbation.law_explanation: 15 CFR § 732.3(b)(3) places the responsibility on the exporter to determine if a license is required. By making export compliance optional, the modified text allows for potential violations of export regulations, as the distributor might not take necessary steps to ensure compliance, and misinterpreting export control laws. Since the contract is between US-based companies this change makes for an obvious contradiction.

Skip to main content Export Administration Regulations (EAR) This tool helps you navigate the Export Administration Regulations (EAR) under 15 CFR 730-774. Commerce Control List (CCL) The Commerce Control List (CCL) (Supplement No. 1 to Part 774 of the EAR) is divided into ten broad categories, and each category is further subdivided into five product groups. Additionally, you may explore our interactive tool . Category 0 - Nuclear Materials Facilities & Equipment [and Miscellaneous Items] Category 1 - Special Materials and Related Equipment, Chemicals, “Microorganisms,” and “Toxins” Category 2 - Materials Processing Category 3 - Electronics Category 4 - Computers Category 5 Part 1 - Telecommunications Category 5 Part 2 - Information Security Category 6 - Sensors and Lasers Category 7 - Navigation and Avionics Category 8 - Marine Category 9 - Aerospace and Propulsion Legal limitations of the tools on the BIS website The tools on the BIS website are not intended to replace the official rules found in the Code of Federal Regulations , nor are they a primer on how to get started. For that introductory guidance, please visit our Learn and Support page. The tools are an editorial compilation of material and not an official legal edition of the Code of Federal Regulations or the Federal Register . We have made every effort to ensure the material presented in these tools are accurate, but if you are relying on them for legal research you should consult the official editions of those sources to confirm your findings. Nothing in these tools binds BIS or creates any rights, benefits, or defenses, substantive or procedural, that are enforceable by any party in any manner. If you find any errors, please email us.
perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates compliance with export restrictions, whereas the changed contract makes compliance optional. This contradicts the responsibility placed on exporters to determine if a license is required as explained in the Law Explanation. The scraped snippets support the existence of Export Administration Regulations (EAR) under 15 CFR 730-774, indicating a legal framework for export controls. The change from 'shall comply' to 'should consider complying' weakens the legally binding nature of the clause, which creates a contradiction.
